one_line_ask: Run a pre-release audio QC pass on the three raw Northbound Signal mic recordings — audit each for hiss, clipping, uneven levels and dead-air against the delivery spec, produce one denoised reference track for A/B comparison, and return a timestamped, per-episode audio QC findings report ranking each defect by severity.
brief: Northbound Signal is a fictional independent interview podcast produced by Meridian Audio Collective — long-form conversations with founders and makers, released weekly on YouTube and the usual podcast platforms. We recorded three new episodes back-to-back and the raw mic tracks are captured separately from the cameras (a single mixed-down mono/stereo voice file per episode, roughly 28-32 minutes each). Before these go to our editor for the final cut, we need a fresh set of ears to tell us exactly what's wrong with the audio so nothing embarrassing ships. Our last drop had a whole segment where the guest's lav was clipping on every laugh and we only caught it after publish, plus one episode had an HVAC hum under the whole intro. We are NOT asking you to edit or master the episodes — that is our editor's job on the timeline. We want an audit. You will receive: (1) three raw voice recordings — ep01_raw_mix.wav (host + guest, guest mic runs hot with intermittent clipping on peaks), ep02_raw_mix.wav (steady room hiss / HVAC hum under the whole thing, low broadband noise floor), ep03_raw_mix.wav (badly unbalanced — host is loud, guest is quiet, plus two long dead-air gaps where someone
